data.title: Top SA Quant rated credit card issuers as Trump calls for 10% interest rate cap
data.description:
President Donald Trump late on Friday called for a one-year cap on credit card interest rates at 10%, taking aim at issuers for letting Americans be "ripped off" by charging higher rates.

His comments were met with immediate opposition from major banking groups.

"Here is the reality of capping credit card interest rates at 10%. Lenders and consumer finance platforms like (SOFI), (AXP), (COF), (Discover), (NU), and (MELI), along with issuing banks such as (JPM), (BAC), (C), and (WFC), rely on risk-based pricing to cover credit losses, funding costs, fraud, and operating margins. Cap rates too low, and large portions of unsecured consumer credit immediately become uneconomic," Sam Badawi, founder & CEO of Solid Finance, said on X.

"When lenders cannot price for risk, losses rise and credit availability contracts. That hits earnings, pressures stock prices, and eventually forces companies to raise debt or equity at unfavorable terms. This is not consumer protection. It is a structural shock to the credit system, and the downstream consequences would be severe," Badawi added.

The U.S. market has several credit card issuers for consumers. See below J.D. Power's 2025 customer satisfaction ranking of issuers:

Seeking Alpha also compiled a list of credit card issuers ranked by their Quant rating.

The list is topped by Bank of America (BAC), with a Quant Rating of 4.96, followed by Capital One (COF) at 4.94 and PNC Financial Services (PNC) at 4.78. These three stocks are the only ones on the list to earn a Strong Buy rating. The remaining eight stocks on the list are rated Hold, starting with U.S. Bancorp (USB) at 3.47 and ending with Citigroup at 3.19.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Ratings grade stocks on their relative performance on various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered a bullish rating and any rating of 2.5 or below considered a bearish rating.

Here is the list:

  1. Bank of America (BAC), Quant Rating: 4.96, Strong Buy; +1.55% YTD.
  2. Capital One (COF), Quant Rating: 4.94, Strong Buy; +2.82% YTD.
  3. PNC (PNC), Quant Rating: 4.78, Strong Buy; +4.23% YTD.
  4. U.S. Bank (USB), Quant Rating: 3.47, Hold; +3.47% YTD.
  5. Truist (TFC), Quant Rating: 3.43, Hold; +2.87% YTD.
  6. Wells Fargo (WFC), Quant Rating: 3.42, Hold; +2.95% YTD.
  7. JPMorgan (JPM), Quant Rating: 3.41, Hold; +2.16% YTD.
  8. American Express (AXP), Quant Rating: 3.40, Hold; +1.53% YTD.
  9. TD Bank (TD), Quant Rating: 3.36, Hold; +0.08% YTD.
  10. Fifth Third (FITB), Quant Rating: 3.26, Hold; +5.26% YTD.
  11. Citi (C), Quant Rating: 3.19, Hold; +3.97% YTD.

Here are some financial sector-focused exchange-traded funds: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO).

data.title: Trump signs executive order to block claims on Venezuelan oil revenue
data.description:
President Donald Trump signed an executive order using his emergency powers to prevent creditors from having legal claims on Venezuelan oil revenue held in U.S. Treasury accounts, the White House said on Saturday.

The directive came as Trump wrapped up a meeting with top executives of more than 20 U.S. energy companies on Friday, nearly a week after the U.S., in a dramatic pre-dawn operation, captured Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro in Caracas.

Exxon Mobil (XOM), Chevron (CVX), and ConocoPhillips (COP) were among the notable attendees at the event.

The directive, based on the 1977 International Emergency Economic Powers Act and the 1976 National Emergencies Act, says that “the Foreign Government Deposit Funds constitute property of the Government of Venezuela and do not constitute the property of any private party.”

A White House Fact Sheet read, "President Trump is preventing the seizure of Venezuelan oil revenue that could undermine critical U.S. efforts to ensure economic and political stability in Venezuela." 

Energy: Natural gas (NG1:COM), Nymex crude (CL1:COM), Brent crude (CO1:COM)

Related ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: "Big Short" investor Michael Burry bets against Oracle
data.description:
• Famed investor Michael Burry has disclosed that he currently owns put options on Oracle (ORCL), marking his latest bearish bet in the booming field of artificial intelligence. He attributed the decision to uncertainty over Oracle’s (ORCL) cloud strategy and its rising debt load.
  • Burry, who accurately predicted the U.S. housing sector collapse nearly two decades ago, made the disclosure late Friday in a Substack feed, which he launched in November as a paid newsletter after liquidating his hedge fund, Scion Asset Management.
  • Illustrated in Michael Lewis's book "The Big Short" and its film adaptation, Burry's short bets on subprime mortgage securities paid off before the U.S. housing market collapsed, leading to the 2008 financial crisis.
  • Early November, Nvidia (NVDA) and Palantir (PLTR) traded lower after Scion Asset Management disclosed put options on the companies.

data.title: Musk asks why U.K. is “so fascist” amid backlash over Grok’s AI images
data.description:
Elon Musk on Saturday suggested that the U.K. government is being “fascist” amid allegations that his social media site X’s artificial intelligence ((AI)) chatbot Grok was being used to digitally undress people without their consent.

In a social media post, the billionaire investor and CEO of the EV maker Tesla (TSLA) and space exploration company SpaceX (SPACE) said, “Why is the UK government so fascist?” His post included a table highlighting the U.K. as the country with the most arrests tied to online comments.

His comments came after the British Technology Secretary Liz Kendall called for urgent action against xAI (X.AI), which owns Musk's social media platform X, after reports indicated that Grok was being extensively used to generate sexually manipulating images of women and children.

“Sexually manipulating images of women and children is despicable and abhorrent. It is an insult and totally unacceptable for Grok to still allow this if you’re willing to pay for it,” Kendall said, adding that she would back regulator Ofcom if it bans U.K. access to X, which runs Grok.

" I, and more importantly the public, would expect to see Ofcom update on next steps in days, not weeks," Kendall noted.

data.title: Real estate stocks see the impact of Trump's housing relief push this week
data.description:
Real estate stocks showed a mixed performance this week, with returns continuing to vary across property sectors.

Single-family home rental REITs and several real estate service providers sank after President Donald Trump said on Wednesday he'll take steps to ban large institutional investors from buying single-family homes in his effort to make housing more affordable for Americans.

Invitation Homes (INVH) fell 4.40% during the week to close at $26.50, while American Homes 4 Rent (AMH) was down 2.82% to $31.02. CBRE Group (CBRE) closed the week 3.29% lower at $165.46.

Meanwhile, a number of housing-sensitive fintech stocks jumped on the back of another of Trump's efforts to bring down housing costs. 

Trump on Thursday said he's instructing unnamed representatives to acquire $200B in mortgage bonds. His move is aimed at narrowing mortgage spreads and lowering borrowing costs -- which could be a boon for loan origination volume and transaction demand for related companies.

Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) advanced 20.10% on a weekly basis to close at $7.29, while Offerpad Solutions (OPAD) added 64.66% to $2.19.

Similarly, homebuilder stocks were trading higher on Friday after a media report about Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick meeting executives from major companies in the sector. LGI Homes (LGIH) +27.74% and Hovnanian Enterprises (HOV) +23.44% stood out as winners of the week.

Additionally, the sector saw a significant number of capital-raising announcements this week.

Realty Income's (O) $750M convertible senior notes offering, Simon Property Group (SPG)'s $800M debt offering, Compass's (COMP) $850M convertible senior notes offering, Vornado's (VNO) $500M 7-year bond offering, and Tanger's (SKT) $220M exchangeable senior notes offering were to name a few.

Winners & Losers

Among largecap stocks, CoStar Group (CSGP) led the weekly losers. The Arlington-based company lost ~11% value from the prior week to close at $58.49 after delivering 2026 adjusted earnings per diluted share guidance below the Wall Street consensus.

SBA Communications (SBAC) (-5.86% W/W to $181.20) and Crown Castle (CCI) (-5.67% W/W to $83.67) followed. On Friday, the two telecom tower REIT stocks, widely considered to be sensitive to carrier capex trends, hit their lowest level in 52 weeks.

In the category, Weyerhaeuser (WY) (+8.11% W/W to $25.73), Public Storage (PSA) (+7.63% W/W to $278.15), and Iron Mountain (IRM) (+7.15% W/W to $89.19) were the top gainers.

For the midcap stocks, Compass (COMP) was the top performer with a 22.29% weekly gain to $12.84. The New York-based real estate services provider had been on a winning streak after announcing shareholders approval for Anywhere Real Estate (HOUS) merger.

HOUS led the smallcap gainers of the week, adding 24.58% from the previous week to go defunct as a trading symbol on Friday.

Greenwood Village-based homebuilder Century Communities (CCS) (+15.93% W/W to $68.33) was another significant gainer among the midcap stocks.

On the flip side, Agree Realty (ADC) (-2.23% W/W to $70.51) was the bottom performer in the category. On Monday, the Royal Oak-based retail REIT issued a Q4 2025 investment volume guidance below the prior-quarter level.

For the smallcap losers, Los Angeles-based Hudson Pacific Properties (HPP) led the pack, retreating 13.52% from last week to close at $9.72. The stock hit a new 52-week low on Friday, having secured a downgrade to Market Perform (Hold-equivalent) from Outperform (Buy-equivalent) by BMO Capital and the price target reduced to $11 from $16.

Here is a look at the subsector performances for the week:

data.title: Insider trades: Block, Broadcom, Micron among notable names this week
data.description:
Insider activity was relatively light during the first full trading week of 2026, with transactions seen across companies like Broadcom (AVGO) and Micron Technology (MU). The following transactions occurred between January 5 and January 9.

  • Broadcom (AVGO) insider sold shares worth $45.16M in the week. The largest sale came from President and CEO Hock Tan, who  sold shares worth $24.31M. Tan let go of 70,000 shares at $347.30 each, reducing his holdings to 908,474 shares. Meanwhile, Chief Financial Officer Kirsten Spears and Chief Legal Officer Mark Brazeal sold shares worth around $10M.
  • Micron Technology’s (MU) Chief Accounting Officer Scott Allen  sold 2,000 shares at an average price of $337.50 per share, for a total value of $675,000. Following the transaction, Allen now holds 38,237 Micron shares.
  • Block (XYZ) also saw multiple executives report their sales transactions last week. CFO and COO Amrita Ahuja sold 1,101 shares at $63.98 per share, totaling roughly $70,442, and now owns 271,864 shares. Chief Legal Officer Christy Esperanza sold 331 shares at the same price for about $21,177, leaving her with 122,867 shares. Business Lead Owen Jennings sold 822 shares at $63.98 per share for about $ 52,592 and now holds 242,718 shares of the firm.
  • Snowflake’s (SNOW) Senior Vice President of Engineering and Support Vivek Raghunathan  sold 11,801 shares at an average price of $220.44 per share, for a total value of about $2.60M. Following the sale, Raghunathan now holds 218,020 Snowflake shares.
  • Marvell Technology’s (MRVL) Chief Legal Officer Mark Casper  divested 5,000 shares at an average price of $93.08 per share, amounting to around $465,400. Casper now owns 19,418 Marvell shares.
  • Several Directors at Simon Property Group (SPG)  purchased shares in the past week, for a total value of $407,712. Combined, the insiders bought a total of 2,192 shares of the company.
  • Fred Cohen, director at Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA),  purchased shares worth $1.40M in the company. Cohen bought 150,000 shares for $9.35 each, increasing his holdings in the company to 207,453 shares.

data.title: Bessent says U.S. Treasury can easily pay for any tariff refunds
data.description:
With the U.S. Supreme Court expected to decide on the legality of reciprocal tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told Reuters that the government has more than enough funds to cover any tariff refunds in case of an unfavorable ruling.

His remarks came after the court held off a widely anticipated ruling on Trump’s tariffs on Friday in a case closely followed by Costco (COST) and other major companies, which are also involved in the legal fight seeking to benefit from potential refunds.

"It won't be a problem if we have to do it, but I can tell you that if it happens, which I don't think it's going to—it's just a corporate boondoggle," Bessent said during an interview with Reuters on Friday.  

With nearly $774B of cash on hand as of Thursday, the Treasury can easily cover any refunds, which, according to data from the Customs and Border Protection Agency, could approach $150B based on duties tied to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The case challenges Trump's decision to use IEEPA to impose tariffs on nearly all trading partners of the U.S. in April

However, Bessent said that in his opinion, the longer the ruling gets delayed, the more likely it is that the court will rule in favor of the government.

"We're not talking about the money all goes out in a day. Probably over weeks, months, may take over a year, right?" he added.

"Costco, who's suing the U.S. government, are they going to give the money back to their clients?" he argued, noting that companies mostly were not passing tariffs to consumers. There was "very, very little, if any, pass-through," he said.

According to a Bloomberg analysis, other publicly traded firms suing the Trump administration over tariffs include BorgWarner (BWA), Standex International (SXI), Teladoc Health (TDOC), Moog (MOG.A) (MOG.B),  Astronics (ATRO), G-III Apparel Group (GII), Lifetime Brands (LCUT), Plexus (PLXS), Goodyear (GT), Weyco Group (WEYS) and EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF) (ESLOY).

Dole (DOLE) and e.l.f. Cosmetics (ELF) were among the latest to file lawsuits against tariffs this year. Subsidiaries of large corporations, such as Alcoa (AA), Yokohama Rubber (YORUF) (YORUY), Kawasaki Heavy Industries (KWHIY) (KWHIF) have also joined the legal battle.

data.title: FIGR, UWMC climb, ETOR, AIG retreat in week's financials roundup
data.description:
Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) rose the most of any financial stock (with market cap over $2B) this week, surging 33%;

UWM Holdings (UWMC) took the second slot, jumping 22%; as a number of real estate and fintech stock rallied after President Donald Trump's latest housing relief effort;

Sezzle (SEZL) gapped up 19%; and

Rocket Companies (RKT), another housing-sensitive fintech, and Hut 8 (HUT) each gained 17%.

On the negative side, eToro (ETOR) slid 11% in the same week Goldman downgraded the trading platform to Neutral from Buy on growing competition;

American International Group (AIG) also shed 11% after announcing the retirement of Peter Zaffino from the CEO post by mid-2026.

India's HDFC Bank (HDB) fell 9.3%;

MarketAxess Holdings (MKTX) dipped 5.5%; and

HCI (HCI) was down 5.4%.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Palantir, Merck and Lockheed Martin among top picks
data.description:
The S&P500 (SP500) closed in the green on Friday, with Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) rising 0.4% and 0.6%, respectively, for the week. Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts.

Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Lockheed Martin gets bullish rating from Truist; Northrop Grumman downgraded

Truist Securities upgraded Lockheed Martin (LMT) to Buy from Hold with a $605 price target, raised from $500, saying the company's FY 2025 underperformance coupled with attractive valuation creates a compelling risk/reward profile in 2026.

Analyst Michael Ciarmoli also touted Lockheed's missile portfolio as well-positioned to take advantage of missile demand across its top programs as well as sees stability and perhaps modest opportunities for the F-35.

However, Ciarmoli downgraded Northrop Grumman (NOC) to Hold from Buy with a $623 PT, reduced from $688, as he believes the company will maintain its leading prime positions across the nuclear triad and various other in-demand platforms and capabilities while seeing some limitations to further upside to financials over the next 12-24 months.

Jefferies upgrades IBM due to expected software growth in 2026

IBM (IBM) was upgraded to Buy from Neutral by Jefferies as it believes IBM is slated to benefit from software growth in 2026.

Jefferies analyst Brent Thill said macro environment is turning more positive, and tech transformation & accelerating Al adoption trends are driving broad-based demand.

"Management's tone has shifted and remained more optimistic vs. early '25, underpinned by robust organic software growth, synergy capture from M&A, and strategic wins in GenAl consulting engagements,” added Thill.

Palantir gets Buy rating on government, enterprise AI adoption opportunities

Palantir (PLTR) received a Buy rating from Truist Securities as it initiated coverage of the analytics and data-driven software firm, and set a $223 price target on the stock.

"We acknowledge the significant valuation premium PLTR commands, but continue to view it as a Buy given its significant opportunity to drive GenAI adoption for governments & enterprises," said Truist analyst Arvind Ramnani.

Ramnani added that PLTR has seen “material improvement in its momentum driven by the release of AIP, with top-line growth accelerating to 63% y/y from 13% y/y as of 2Q23 - with a larger portion of this growth flowing down to op. margins, reaching 50%+ margins.”

Wolfe upgrades Merck to Outperform; AbbVie downgraded

Wolfe Research upgraded the pharma giant Merck (MRK) to Outperform from Peer Perform, while AbbVie (ABBV) was downgraded to Peer Perform from Outperform.

Analyst Alexandria Hammond argued that Rahway, Merck is well-positioned to face the upcoming loss of market exclusivity for its intravenous version of its blockbuster cancer therapy, Keytruda. She set a $135 per share target on MRK.

For AbbVie, the analyst opined that the company shares already reflect outperformance in its newer immunology products, Skyrizi and Rinvoq, which dominate its immunology and inflammation ((I&I)) franchise following the patent cliff for the arthritis blockbuster Humira. She removed her $275 per-share price target on the stock.

Costco added to top picks list by Mizuho

Mizuho added Costco Wholesale (COST) to its top picks list on the expectation that the sluggish 2025 share price performance is now in the rearview mirror.

Analyst David Bellinger and his team upgraded the retailer to Outperform after having the retailer slotted at Neutral and boosted its PT to $1,000 from $950.

"Shares have corrected ~20% on concerns that both membership and comp sales growth are slowing as part of some underlying change within the COST model," said Bellinger. It was also noted that domestic renewal rates remain exceptionally high at higher than 90% and above the 10-year running average.

Adobe (ADBE) was downgraded to Hold from Buy by Jefferies as the brokerage said it has not noted any revenue contribution boost from Adobe's AI tools, including its full-year 2026 initial guidance.

BMO also downgraded Adobe to Market Perform from Outperform and reduced PT to $375 from $400. as the brokerage believes shares will remain range-bound due to increasing competition and no perceptible positive catalysts on the horizon.

Susquehanna upgraded Caesars Entertainment (CZR) to a Positive rating after having the casino stock slotted at Neutral. As part of its valuation call, the firm maintains that the stock’s risk-reward profile is now too attractive to ignore at $23.

Twilio (TWLO) was downgraded by Piper Sandler, with analyst James Fish citing concerns about valuation. In addition, Fish tweaked PT to $148 from $145.

data.title: SpaceX, NASA schedule first-ever space medical evacuation
data.description:
Following a medical issue faced by a crew member, NASA and SpaceX (SPACE) will take steps to bring back four astronauts from the International Space Station this week, marking the first medical evacuation in the history of the orbiting lab.

In a statement late Friday, NASA said that the undocking of the agency’s SpaceX Crew-11 mission from the International Space Station is scheduled to take place no earlier than 5 p.m. ET, Jan. 14, subject to favorable weather conditions.

Two NASA astronauts, Zena Cardman and Mike Fincke; Japanese astronaut Kimiya Yui; and Russian cosmonaut Oleg Platanov will return to earth aboard SpaceX's (SPACE) Crew-11 Dragon spacecraft to splash down off the coast of California at roughly 3:40 a.m. on Jan. 15.

“Dragon and Crew-11 are targeted to undock from the space station no earlier than Wednesday, January 14,” SpaceX (SPACE) said in an X post.

On Thursday, NASA announced that it was planning an earlier-than-expected end to Crew-11’s mission due to a medical issue involving a single crew member whose situation is stable. It will be the first time in the space station’s 25-year history that a medical incident has forced NASA to cut short a crewed mission already in space.
